data: Accademia nazionale dei Lincei, Istituto nazionale di archeologia e storia dell’arte (Italy), e Reale Accademia d’Italia, «Notizie degli scavi di antichità», Notizie degli Scavi di Antichità, 1876, http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/1646037

Large irregular but broadly rectangular block of grey limestone (of moderate quality, not seemingly the compact Taormina limestone), damaged on all sides, especially upper and lower right. It is unclear whether either the top or the bottom of the text is preserved, but a clear vacat is visible at the end of lines 2, 3, and 4, although the stone is not intact on the right..

Large, regular letters of generally fairly broad module, but with some irregularlity in the width of the module. Lunate epsilon and sigma, uncial omega. Large round omicron. narrow alpha with broken bar.

Found during works to open Via del Littorio on Ortygia, between 1934 and 1936; one of a number of unstratified finds (apparently in secondary deposition, reused as building material) in the area of Largo Antonello Gagini, formerly Largo XXVIII Ottobre. Now in storage in the Museo Archeologico Regionale di Siracusa, magazzino C.
I.Sicily with the permission of the Assessorato Regionale dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana - Dipartimento dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana; photo J. Prag 2022-04-26

An outstanding collection of archaeological material. The collection ranges from Siracusa and the surrounding territory (Megara Hyblaia, Akrai, Kasmenai etc) to encompass all of eastern Sicily, reaching as far as Gela and Agrigento. The ground floor covers prehistory through to the end of the Classical period. Upstairs includes Hellenistic and Roman Syracuse and a display of material from the catacombs. A gallery displaying material from Hellenistic eastern Sicily is in preparation.
Association pour l’encouragement des études greques, « Bulletin épigraphique », Revue des études grecques, 1888, http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/797735566
Ricerche di antichità e di epigrafia siceliote

La Sicilia da Sesto Pompeo a Diocleziano
Giacomo Manganaro, «La Sicilia da Sesto Pompeo a Diocleziano», Aufstieg und Niedergang der romischen Welt 2.11.1 (1988): 3–89
Iscrizioni, epigrafi ed epigrammi in Greco della Sicilia orientale di epoca romana
Giacomo Manganaro, «Iscrizioni, epigrafi ed epigrammi in Greco della Sicilia orientale di epoca romana», Mélanges de l’École Francaise de Rome: Antiquité 106, fasc. 1 (1994): 79–118
Fontane ed edifici termali nella Catina « bilingue » tardo-antica e l’editto di Eumathios del 434 d. C.

Giacomo Manganaro, «Fontane ed edifici termali nella Catina “ bilingue ” tardo-antica e l’editto di Eumathios del 434 d. C.», Travaux et mémoires du Centre de recherches d’histoire et civilisation byzantines 16 (2010): 513–31

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

  • Text of Guarducci

translation

commentary

Understood by Guarducci (ed.pr.) to be a verse epigram for a doctor, likely of the second century CE, of a type familiar in mainland Italy, and perhaps to be associated with a sanctuary of Aesclepias in the vicinity (a statue of Hygeia was found in the area also). Manganaro instead suggested an interpretation relating to the construction of a thermal spa / bath building, perhaps in the fourth century (strongly rejected by Robert). ISic000632 was found in close proximity.

Text of Guarducci

Understood by Guarducci (ed.pr.) to be a verse epigram for a doctor, likely of the second century CE, of a type familiar in mainland Italy, and perhaps to be associated with a sanctuary of Aesclepias in the vicinity (a statue of Hygeia was found in the area also). Manganaro instead suggested an interpretation relating to the construction of a thermal spa / bath building, perhaps in the fourth century (strongly rejected by Robert). ISic000632 was found in close proximity.
